description:
In SocketManager.unregister, when we mark connection as unregistered=true and remove it from array, then decrement IP count within the same mutex block, there's still a potential race with the writer_fiber that also calls unregister_connection and tries to decrement IP count.

Even though the code has decrement_ip_count_locked with negative count detection, the issue is that we mark unregistered=true but don't prevent the writer_fiber from calling unregister_connection. The writer_fiber will see unregistered=true and skip its own decrement, but by that time we may have already decremented.

However, looking at the code more carefully - the issue is in cleanup_dead_connections which calls unregister_connection directly without setting the unregistered flag. This means cleanup_dead_connections could double-decrement if unregister is called between the websocket.closed? check and the actual cleanup.

Location: src/websocket/socket_manager.cr
title: Bug: SocketManager IP tracking double-decrement protection flawed
